La ilaha illal-lah, wahdahu la shareeka lah, lahul -mulku walahul -hamd, yuhyee wayumeetu wahuwa hayyun la yamoot, biyadihil -khayru wahuwa AAal a kulli shayin qadeer.

“None has the right to be worshipped except All ah, alone, without partner, to Him

belongs all sovereignty and praise . He gives life and causes death, and He is livin g and does not die . In His hand is all good and He is over all things, omnipotent.”

4¥. Supplication for when the mounted animal(or mean of transport) stumbles

Bismil-lah. “In the name of All ah.’

wiles ail p85 GLELA, Gls alll yd AstawdiAAul -laha deenak, wa -amanatak, wakhaw ateema AAamalik .

‘I place your religion, your faithfulness and the ends of your deeds in the trust of Allah.’

SS Lia A 29 SS Hy gs sll DS Zawwadakal -lahut-taqwa, waghafara thanbak, wayassara lakal -khayra haythuma kunt. “May Allah endow you with taqwa, forgive your sins and facilitate all good for you, wherever you be .” taqwa: a comprehensive term which refers to all good actions and obedience i.e. performing the commanded actions and avoiding the prohibited actions 4%, Remembrance while ascending or descending (m2)

Jabir - said: While ascending, we would say:

Allahu akbar . “Allah is the greatest .’ ...and when descending, we would say:

ail Olathe Subhanal-lah. “How perfect All ah is.’ 4Y. Prayer of the traveller as dawn approaches (wet) AD Ge aah

SamiAAa s amiAAun bi hamdil-lahi wahusni bala-ihi AAalayn a. Rabbana sahibna waafdil AAalayn a AAa-ithan bill ahi minan -nar. “May a witness, be witness to our praise of All ah for His fav ours and bounties upon us. Our Lord, protect us, show favour on us and deliver us from every evil . I take refuge in All ah from the fire .” 4A, Stopping or lodging somewhere

(GS be “335 Gye SALE all) GLK Sb gel

aAAoo thu bikalim atil-lahit-tammati min sharri ma khalaq.

‘I take refuge in All ah’s perfect words from the evil that He has created 44, While returning from travel

Ibn AAumar .# reported that the Messenger of Allah 2 on return from a battle or from performing the pilgrimage would say at every high point:

Allahu akbar, All ahu akbar, All ahu akbar.

“Allah is the greatest, All ah is the greatest, All ah is the greatest .” ...and then he would say: cpr ech US Cle “phy cLeall AL, GLa AI A GL yt Y GONG al Y) ally aR y odie “peas code ’y dil Gide cysdale LO! Osrle Gs Gal tas GI SY

La ilaha illal-lahu wahdahu la shareeka lah, lahul -mulku walahul -hamd, wahuwa AAala kulli shay-in qadeer, ayiboona ta-iboon, AA abidoon, lirabbin a hamidoon, sadaqal-lahu waAAdah, wana sara AAabdah, wahazamal -ahzaba wahdah. “None has the right to be worshipped except All ah, alone, without partner . To Him belongs all sovereignty and praise, and He is over al | things omnipotent . We return, repent, worship and praise our Lord . Allah fulfilled His promise, aided His Servant, and single -handedly defeated the allies .” \++, What to say upon receiving pleasing or displeasing news (v4) When he used to receive pleasant news, he would say:

lala 6 ta gill a Aa Alhamdu lill ahil-lathee biniAAmatihi tatimmu s-salihat.

“All Praise is for All ah by whose favour good works are accomplished ...and upon receiving displeasing news, he would say:

Alhamdu lillahi AAala kulli hal.

“All Praise is for All ah in all circumstances .”

\+‘, Excellence of sending prayers upon the Prophet

The Prophet said: ‘Whoever sends a prayer upon me, All ah sends ten upon h im.’

He # also said: ‘Do not take my grave as a place of habitual ceremony. Send prayers upon me, for verily your prayers reach me wherever you are.’

He # also said: ‘A miser is one whom when I am mentioned to him, fails to send prayers upon me.’

\+¥. Excellence of spreading the Ishmic greeting

The Messenger of Allah # said: ‘You shall not enter paradise until you believe, and you shall not believe until you love one another . Shall I not inform you of something, if you were to act upon it, you will indeed achieve mutual love for one another? Spread the greeting amongst yourselves.’

AAammar # said: ‘Three characteristics, whoever combines them, has completed his faith: to be just, to spread greetings to all people and to spend (charitably) out of the little you have.’

Allahu akbar, All ahu akbar, All ahu akbar .

“Allah is the greatest, All ah is the greatest, All ah is the greatest.” ..and then he would say the following three times making a supplication (one should make a personal supplication) after each time: AY) Uo yaa8 Sk US (le “gh g Mea AY, Gaal) 40 A) GL pk Sak 2d OH al) aay GI jal jh 3s Odie “peg code 5 “jal OO, al UY

La ilaha illal-lahu wahdahu la shareeka lah, lahul -mulku walahul -hamd, wahuwa AAala kulli shayin qadeer, | a ilaha illal-lahu wahdah, anjaza waAAdah, wana sara AAabdah, wahazamal -ahzaba wahdah. “None has the right to be worshipped except All ah, alone, without partner .To Him belongs all sovereignty and praise and He is over all things amnipotent _. None has the right to be worshipped except All ah alone. He fulfilled His promise, aided His Servant and single -handedly defeated the allies .” ..he #would repeat this action at Marwah . \+4, The Day of AAarafah ary ‘The best of supplications is the supplication on the day of AAarafah and the best which I and the Prophets before me have said (is):

yok of 28 US (le gh g Meal AL, Gal AD AGL Sa, da a) La ilaha illal-lahu wahdahu la shareeka lah, lahul -mulku walahul -hamd, wahuwa AAala kulli shayin qadeer . “None has the right to be worshipped except All ah, alone, without partner . To Him belongs all praise and sovereignty and He is over all things omnipotent.”

\\+, At the Sacred Site(Al-MashAAar AFHaram)

Jabir . said: ‘He # rode Al-Qaswa until he reached Al-MashAAar Al-Haram, he then faced the qiblah, supplicated to Allah, and extoled His greatness and oneness. He stood until the sun shone but left before it rose.’

Al-Qaswa: The name of the Prophet ’s & camel.

\\‘, When throwing each pebble at the Jamarat

i.e. Stoning the three areas at Mina during Hajj .

Every time the Prophet #8 threw a pebble at any of the three jamar at, he would say: Allahu akbar .

“Allah is the greatest ’

...on completion of the first jamarah, he advanced a little, stood facing the qibl ah, raised his hands and supplicated. He also did this after the second jamarah but not the third.’

\\Y, At the black stone

‘The Prophet 2 circled the KaAAbah on a camel, every time he reached the black stone he would point to it with his staff and say :

“Allah is the greatest ’

\\¥. Supplication made against an enemy (vy)

pels ee jal aelll IGS YI a 5a) Calas ay pee UES 58s pe tll Allahumma munzilal -kitab, sareeAAal -hisab, ihzimil-ahzab, allahummah -zimhum wazalzilhum . “O Allah, Revealer of the Book, Swift at reckoning, defeat the confederates .O Allah, defeat them and convulse them .” \\£, What to say when in fear of a people

(yyy) Allahummak -fineehim bim a shi/t.

“O Allah, protect me from them with what You choose

Subhanal-lah!. “How perfect All ah is.’

(vya) Vs) ail Allahu akbar . “Allah is the greatest .’ \‘\. What to do upon receiving pleasant news (Ps)

The Prophet # would prostrate in gratitude to Allah lei 3s Ayki upon receiving news which pleased him or which caused pleasure . \‘\V, What to say and do when feeling some pain in the body ( rry) ‘Place your hand at the site of the pain and say : Bismil-lah (three times) “In the name of All ah’ (three times) ...the supplicate seven times: (Cho aa) SLAY, Sel La i Ga a, aly 8 gl aAAoo thu billahi waqudratih min sharri m a ajidu wa -ohathir. (seven times). ‘I take refuge in All ah and within His omnipotence from the evil that I feel and am wary of.’ (seven times) \‘\A, What to say when in fear of afflicting something or someone with onés eye The Evil Eye: To look at something and be impressed wit h it, causing harm to befall it . This “looking” may or may not involve jealousy, and can occur unintentionally, indeed be part of a person’s nature! A person can even inflict harm on himself. From the supplications for the protection against the Evil Eye Ade yt eal
